nodes.label: Kinematics
nodes.subject: Physics
nodes.description: Study of motion of points, bodies, and systems of bodies without considering the forces that cause them to move.

nodes.label: Laws of Motion
nodes.subject: Physics
nodes.description: Newton's three laws relating the motion of a body and the forces acting on it.

nodes.label: Work and Energy
nodes.subject: Physics
nodes.description: Relationship between mechanical work, kinetic energy, potential energy, and energy conservation principles.

nodes.label: Gravitation
nodes.subject: Physics
nodes.description: Newtonian gravity, universal gravitational constant, orbits, and Kepler's laws.

nodes.label: Thermodynamics
nodes.subject: Physics
nodes.description: Heat, work, temperature, and statistical behavior of energy systems governed by the laws of thermodynamics.

nodes.label: Modern Physics
nodes.subject: Physics
nodes.description: Quantum mechanics, wave-particle duality, atomic structure, and special relativity.

nodes.label: Sets and Functions
nodes.subject: Mathematics
nodes.description: Basic set operations, union, intersection, functions, domain, range, and mappings.

nodes.label: Algebra
nodes.subject: Mathematics
nodes.description: Solving linear and quadratic equations, polynomial functions, and matrix operations.

nodes.label: Coordinate Geometry
nodes.subject: Mathematics
nodes.description: Locating points on a plane, slopes of lines, equations of lines, circles, and conics.

nodes.label: Calculus
nodes.subject: Mathematics
nodes.description: Differentiation, limits, derivatives, integration, and calculating area under curves.

nodes.label: Probability
nodes.subject: Mathematics
nodes.description: Likelihood of events, conditional probability, independent events, Bayes' theorem, and distributions.

nodes.label: Arrays
nodes.subject: Data Structures
nodes.description: Contiguous block of elements indexed from 0 to N-1, supporting constant time access.

nodes.label: Linked Lists
nodes.subject: Data Structures
nodes.description: Sequence of nodes where each node contains data and pointers to the next node.

nodes.label: Stacks
nodes.subject: Data Structures
nodes.description: LIFO (Last In First Out) structure used in recursive call stacks and expression parsing.

nodes.label: Queues
nodes.subject: Data Structures
nodes.description: FIFO (First In First Out) structure used in print queues and CPU process scheduling.

nodes.label: Complexity
nodes.subject: Data Structures
nodes.description: Big O, Big Omega, and Big Theta notation representing computational time and space bounds.

nodes.label: Sorting
nodes.subject: Data Structures
nodes.description: Arranging data in ascending or descending order (Bubble, Insertion, Quick, Merge Sort).

nodes.label: Searching
nodes.subject: Data Structures
nodes.description: Linear and Binary Search algorithms to find target keys in dataset arrays.

nodes.label: Hashing
nodes.subject: Data Structures
nodes.description: Mapping keys to index values using hash functions, collision resolution via chaining/open addressing.

nodes.label: Binary Trees
nodes.subject: Data Structures
nodes.description: Hierarchical node structure where each parent node has at most two child nodes.

nodes.label: AVL Trees
nodes.subject: Data Structures
nodes.description: Self-balancing binary search trees where balance factor difference cannot exceed 1.

nodes.label: Red-Black Trees
nodes.subject: Data Structures
nodes.description: Color-balanced binary search trees ensuring O(log N) operations in worst case.

nodes.label: Heaps
nodes.subject: Data Structures
nodes.description: Max/Min binary heaps representing complete binary trees used for priority queue operations.

nodes.label: Graphs
nodes.subject: Data Structures
nodes.description: Networks of vertices connected by edges, represented by adjacency matrices or lists.

nodes.label: Graph Algorithms
nodes.subject: Data Structures
nodes.description: Dijkstra's shortest path, Kruskal's/Prim's minimum spanning trees, BFS, and DFS traversals.

nodes.label: Dynamic Programming
nodes.subject: Data Structures
nodes.description: Solving subproblems recursively, caching results to avoid recalculating overlapping subproblems.

nodes.label: Shakespearian Plays
nodes.subject: English Literature
nodes.description: Tragedies, Comedies, and Histories penned by William Shakespeare, exploring dramatic arcs.

nodes.label: Literary Devices
nodes.subject: English Literature
nodes.description: Metaphors, Similes, Personification, and Alliteration used to enrich literary meaning.

nodes.label: Romantic Poetry
nodes.subject: English Literature
nodes.description: Focus on nature, personal expression, and emotions in early 19th-century poetry.

nodes.label: Victorian Novels
nodes.subject: English Literature
nodes.description: Social realism, industrial critique, class divides, and moral reform in Victorian-era prose.

nodes.label: Modernist Literature
nodes.subject: English Literature
nodes.description: Break from classical structures, stream-of-consciousness, fragmentation, and alienation.

nodes.label: Tragedy
nodes.subject: English Literature
nodes.description: Genre describing the downfall of a heroic character due to personal flaws or fate.

nodes.label: Existential Drama
nodes.subject: English Literature
nodes.description: Theatre of the absurd, alienation, and questioning of objective truth or meaning.

nodes.label: Satire
nodes.subject: English Literature
nodes.description: Ridiculing vices, conventions, or corrupt systems through irony and exaggeration.

nodes.label: Post-Colonial Criticism
nodes.subject: English Literature
nodes.description: Analyzing literary legacies of colonial rule, displacement, and cultural subversion.
